questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If sound travels in air with the speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>340</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>, then the number of tones present in an open organ pipe of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> for a maximum frequency of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1200</mn><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math>, are
questions.solution:
Given, speed of sound, v=340 m s-1
Length of open organ pipe, l=2 m

Frequency, f=1200 Hz
For open organ pipe,

Fundamental frequency, f0=v2l=3402×2=85

∴ Number of tones present in the open organ pipe
=ff0=120085=14.11≃14
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

l 1 l 2 = 1 2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The displacement y of a particle in a medium can be expressed as, $y=10^{-6} \sin \left(100 t+20 x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) m$ where $t$ is in second and $x$ in meter. The speed of the wave is
questions.solution:
From equation given,
$\begin{array}{l}
\omega=100 \\
\text { and } \mathrm{k}=20 \\
\mathrm{v}=\frac{\omega}{\mathrm{k}}=\frac{100}{20}=5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
\end{array}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniform string is vibrating with a fundamental frequency ' $f$ '. The new frequency, if radius \& length both are doubled would be
questions.solution:
Fundamental frequency of a vibrating string is expressed as
$$
f=\frac{1}{2 L} \sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}}=\frac{1}{L D} \sqrt{\frac{T}{\pi \rho}}
$$
where,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& D= \text { diameter of string } \\
& \rho= \text { density of the } \\
& \text { material of string }
\end{aligned}
$$
As length $L$ and radius are doubled, the new frequency
$$
f^{\prime}=\frac{1}{(2 L)(2 D)} \sqrt{\frac{T}{\pi \rho}}=\frac{1}{4} f
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: When two waves of almost equal frequency $\mathrm{n}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{n}_{2}$ are produced simultaneously, then the time interval between succesive maxima is
questions.solution: Time interval between two suecessive maxima $=$ time interval between two successive beats $=\frac{1}{n_{1}-n_{2}}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If a progressive wave is represented as $y=2 \sin \pi\left(\frac{t}{2}-\frac{x}{4}\right)$ where $\mathrm{x}$ is in metre and $\mathrm{t}$ is in second, then the distance travelled by the wave in $5 \mathrm{~s}$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{l}
\text { Given, } y=3 \sin \pi\left(\frac{t}{2}-\frac{x}{4}\right) \\
=3 \sin \left(\frac{\pi t}{2}-\frac{\pi x}{4}\right)
\end{array}$
Comparing it with standard equation
$\begin{array}{l}
y=r \sin \frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}(v t-x) \\
=r \sin \left(\frac{2 \pi v t}{\lambda}-\frac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}\right)
\end{array}$
We have, $\frac{2 \pi \mathrm{v}}{\lambda}=\frac{\pi}{2}$ or $\mathrm{v}=\frac{\lambda}{4}$
and $\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}=\frac{\pi}{4}$ or $\lambda=8 \mathrm{~m} \therefore \mathrm{v}=\frac{8}{4}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
So, the distance travelled by wave in $t$ second $=\mathrm{vt}=2 \times 5=10 \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The frequency of the second overtone of the open pipe is equal to the frequency of the first overtone of the closed pipe. The ratio of the lengths of the open pipe and the closed pipe is
questions.solution:
The frequency of first overtone for closed pipe,
$f_{c}=\frac{3 \mathrm{v}}{4 I_{c}}$
Given, i.e.,
$\begin{aligned}
f_{c} &=f_{0} \\
\frac{3 v}{4 I_{c}} &=\frac{3 v}{2 I_{o}} \Rightarrow \frac{I_{o}}{I_{c}}=\frac{2}{1}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
The equation of a progressive wave is given by $y=5 \sin (100 \pi t-0.4 \pi x)$ where $y$ and $x$ are in $\mathrm{m}$ and $t$ is in $\mathrm{s}$.
(1) The amplitude of the wave is $5 \mathrm{~m}$.
(2) The wavelength of the wave is $5 \mathrm{~m}$.
(3) The frequency of the wave is $50 \mathrm{~Hz}$.
(4) The velocity of the wave is $250 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$.
Which of the following statements are correct?
questions.solution:
The equation of a given progressive wave is
$$
y=5 \sin (100 \pi t-0.4 \pi x)
$$
The standard equation of a progressive wave is
$$
y=a \sin (\omega t-k x)
$$
Comparing (i) and (ii), we get
$$
a=5 \mathrm{~m}, \omega=100 \pi \mathrm{rad} \mathrm{s}^{-1}, k=0.4 \pi \mathrm{m}^{-1}
$$



(1) Amplitude of the wave, $a=5 \mathrm{~m}$
(2) Wavelength of the wave,
$$
\lambda=\frac{2 \pi}{k}=\frac{2 \pi}{0.4 \pi}=5 \mathrm{~m}
$$
(3) Frequency of the wave,
$$
v=\frac{\omega}{2 \pi}=\frac{100 \pi}{2 \pi}=50 \mathrm{~Hz}
$$
(4) Velocity of the wave,
$$
v=v \lambda=\left(50 \mathrm{~s}^{-1}\right)(5 \mathrm{~m})=250 \mathrm{~m} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: The equation of a progressive wave is $y=4 \sin (4 \pi t-0.04 x+\pi / 3)$ where $x$ is in meter and $t$ is in second. The velocity of the wave is
questions.solution: Hints : Velocity of wave $=\frac{\omega}{\mathrm{K}}=\frac{4 \pi}{0.04}=100 \pi \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A string vibrates with a frequency of $200 \mathrm{~Hz}$. When its length is doubled and tension is altered, it begins to vibrate with a frequency of $300 \mathrm{~Hz}$. The ratio of the new tension to the original tension is
questions.solution:
Given, $L_{2}=2 L_{1}, f_{1}=200 \mathrm{~Hz}, f_{2}=300 \mathrm{~Hz}$
We know that, frequency of vibrating string is given as
$$
f=\frac{1}{2 L} \sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}}
$$
where, $\mu=$ mass per unit length of string
and $L=$ length of the string.
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & f \propto \frac{\sqrt{T}}{L} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{f_{2}}{f_{1}}=\sqrt{\frac{T_{2}}{T_{1}}}, \frac{L_{1}}{L_{2}} \\
\Rightarrow \quad \sqrt{\frac{T_{2}}{T_{1}}}=\frac{f_{2} L_{2}}{f_{1} L_{1}}=\frac{300}{200} \times \frac{2 L_{1}}{L_{1}} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \sqrt{\frac{T_{2}}{T_{1}}}=3 \\
& \frac{T_{2}}{T_{1}}=9 \\
\therefore \quad & T_{2}: T_{1}=9: 1
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: When a string is divided into three segments of lengths $l_1, l_2$ and $l_3$, the fundamental frequencies of these three segments are $v_1$, $v_2$ and $v_3$ respectively. The original fundamental frequency $(v)$ of the string is
questions.solution:
The fundamental frequency of string
$\begin{aligned}
v & =\frac{1}{2 l} \sqrt{\frac{T}{m}} \\
\therefore v_1 l_1 & =v_2 l_2=v_2 l_3=k
\end{aligned}$
From Eq. (i)
$l_1=\frac{k}{v_1}, l_2=\frac{k}{v_2}, l_3=\frac{k}{v_3}$ ...(i)
Original length
$\begin{aligned}
l & =\frac{k}{v} \\
l & =l_1+l_2+l_3 \\
\frac{k}{v} & =\frac{k}{v_1}+\frac{k}{v_2}+\frac{k}{v_3} \\
\frac{1}{v} & =\frac{1}{v_1}+\frac{1}{v_2}+\frac{1}{v_3}
\end{aligned}$
Here,
$\begin{aligned}
& l=l_1+l_2+l_3 \\
& \frac{k}{v}=\frac{k}{v_1}+\frac{k}{v_2}+\frac{k}{v_3} \\
& \frac{1}{v}=\frac{1}{v_1}+\frac{1}{v_2}+\frac{1}{v_3}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two harmonic travelling waves are described by the equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>y</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>k</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>k</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>ω</mi><mi>t</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>ϕ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> The amplitude of the superposed wave is
questions.solution:
y1=asinkx-ωt         ...1

y2=asin-kx+ωt+ϕ          ...2

On adding 1 and 2,

y1+y2=2asinkx-ωt+-kx+ωt+ϕ2

coskx-ωt+kx-ωt-ϕ2

=2asinϕ2cos2(kx+ωt)-ϕ2

Amplitude, Aϕ is a function of phase difference ϕ between constituent of waves

∴   ϕ=2asinϕ2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The velocities of sound at the same temperature in two monoatomic gases of densities $\rho_1$ and $\rho_2$ are $v_1$ and $v_2$ respectively. If $\rho_1 / \rho_2=4$, then the value of $v_1 / v_2$ is
questions.solution:
Velocity of sound in the gas of density $\rho_1$ is $v_1$ and velocity of sound in the gas of density $\rho_2$ is $v_2$.
Velocity of sound in gas, $v=\sqrt{\frac{\gamma P}{\rho}} \propto \sqrt{\frac{1}{\rho}}$.
Therefore $\frac{v_1}{v_2}=\sqrt{\frac{\rho_2}{\rho_1}}=\sqrt{\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{1}{2}$.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: An organ pipe, open from both end produces 5 beats per second when vibrated with a source of frequency $200 \mathrm{~Hz}$. The second harmonic of the same pipes produces 10 beats per second with a source of frequency $420 \mathrm{~Hz}$. The fundamental frequency of organ pipe is
questions.solution:
Let the fundamental frequency of organ pipe be $\mathrm{f}$
Case I : $f=200 \pm 5=205 \mathrm{~Hz}$ or $195 \mathrm{~Hz}$

Case II : frequency of 2 nd harmonic of organ pipe $=2 \mathrm{f}$ (as is clear from the second figure) $2 \mathrm{f}=420 \pm 10$ or $\mathrm{f}=210 \pm 5$ or $\mathrm{f}=205$ or 215
Hence fundamental frequency oforgan pipe $=205 \mathrm{~Hz}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For air at room temperature, the atmospheric pressure is $1.0 \times 10^{5} \mathrm{Nm}^{-2}$ and density of ait is $1.2 \mathrm{kgm}^{-3}$. For a tube of length $1.0 \mathrm{m}$, closed at one end, the lowest frequency $\begin{array}{lllllll}\text { generated is } & 84 & \text { Hz. } & \text { The } & \text { value } & \text { of } \boldsymbol{\gamma}\end{array}$ (ratio of two specific heats) for air is
questions.solution: The tube is closed at one end. So, it will act like a closed organ pipe. The lowest frequency produced in the tube $$ \begin{array}{l} l=\frac{\lambda}{4}, \lambda=4 \\ l=1 \mathrm{m} \\ f=84 \mathrm{Hz} \\ V=n \lambda=84 \times 4 \end{array} $$ When wave propagate in air, the velocity Here, $$ \begin{array}{l} v=\sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}} \\ p=1.0 \times 10^{5} \mathrm{N} / \mathrm{m}^{2} \end{array} $$ $$ \begin{aligned} \rho &=1.2 \mathrm{kg} / \mathrm{m}^{3} \\ V^{2} &=\frac{\gamma P}{\rho} \\ \gamma &=\frac{\mathrm{v}^{2} \cdot \rho}{\mathrm{P}} \\ &=\frac{(84 \times 4)^{2} \times 1.2}{1.0 \times 10^{5}} \\ &=\frac{135472.2}{10^{5}}=1.354 \\ &=1.4 \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The fundamental frequency of an open organ pipe is $300 \mathrm{~Hz}$. The first overtone of this pipe has same frequency as first overtone of a closed organ pipe. If speed of sound is $330 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, then the length of closed organ pipe is
questions.solution:
For open pipe, $\mathrm{n}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2 \ell},$ where $\mathrm{n}_{0}$ is the
fundamental frequency of open pipe. $\therefore \ell=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2 \mathrm{n}}=\frac{330}{2 \times 300}=\frac{11}{20}$
As freq. of 1 st overtone of open pipe $=$ freq. of 1 st overtone of closed pipe
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad 2 \frac{\mathrm{v}}{2 \ell}=3 \frac{\mathrm{v}}{4 \ell^{\prime}} \Rightarrow \ell^{\prime} &=\frac{3 \ell}{4}=\frac{3}{4} \times \frac{11}{20} \\ &=41.25 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A wave travelling in the positive $x$-direction with amplitude $\mathrm{A}=0.2 \mathrm{~m}$, velocity $v=360 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ and wavelength $\lambda$ $=60 \mathrm{~m}$, then the correct expression for the wave is:
questions.solution:
The general equation of wave travelling in $x$-direction is given as
$y=A \sin \left[\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}(V t-x)\right]$
Where $A=0.2 \mathrm{~m}, v=360 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}, \lambda=$ $60 \mathrm{~m}$
$\therefore$ The equation becomes
$\begin{aligned}
& y=0.2 \sin \left[\frac{2 \pi}{60}(360 t-x)\right] \\
& y=0.2 \sin \left[2 \pi\left(6 t-\frac{x}{60}\right)\right]
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two uniform strings $A$ and $B$ made of steel are made to vibrate under the same tension. If the first overtone of $A$ is equal to the second overtone of $B$ and if the radius of $A$ is twice that of $B$, the ratio of the lengths of the strings is :
questions.solution:
Fundamental frequency of string,
$n=\frac{1}{2 l} \sqrt{\frac{T}{m}}$
where, $m=$ mass per unit length of the string
$=\pi r^2 \rho$
$\therefore \quad n=\frac{1}{2 l} \sqrt{\frac{T}{\pi r^2 \rho}}$
For string $A$ first overtone,
$n_A=\frac{1}{l_1} \sqrt{\frac{T_1}{m_1}}=\frac{1}{l_1} \sqrt{\frac{T_1}{\pi r_1^2 \rho}}$
For string $B$ second overtone,
$n_B=\frac{3}{2 l_2} \sqrt{\frac{T_2}{\pi r_2^2 \rho_2}}$
According to question,
$n_A=n_B$
$\frac{1}{l_1} \sqrt{\frac{T_1}{\pi r_1^2 l_1}}=\frac{3}{2 l_2} \sqrt{\frac{T_2}{\pi r_2^2 l_2}}$
but $\quad T_1=T_2$
$\therefore \quad \frac{2 l_2}{3 l_1}=\sqrt{\frac{\pi r_1^2 \rho}{\pi r_2^2 \rho}}$
$\frac{4 l_2^2}{9 l_1^2}=\frac{r_1^2}{r_2^2}$
$\left(\frac{l_1}{l_2}\right)^2=\frac{4 r_2^2}{9 r_1^2}$
$\frac{2 l_2}{3 l_1}=\frac{r_1}{r_2}$
$\frac{l_2}{l_1}=\frac{3 r_1}{2 r_2}$
$\frac{l_1}{l_2}=\frac{2 r_2}{3 r_1}$
but $\quad r_1=2 r_2$
$\therefore \quad \frac{l_1}{l_2}=\frac{2 r_2}{3\left(2 r_2\right)}$
$l_1: l_2=1: 3$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: For a certain organ pipe, three successive resonance frequencies are observed at 425, 595 and 765 Hz, respectively. The length of the pipe is (speed of sound in air = 340 <math><mi>m</mi><msup><mrow><mi>s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> )
questions.solution:
For closed organ pipe, resonant frequency is given by, νn=2n+1V4l, where V&l represents speed of sound and length of organ respectively.

For first case, 
2n+1V4l=425  ...1

For second case,
2n+1+1V4l=595
⇒2n+3V4l=595  ...2
On solving 1&2, we have
⇒2V4l=170
⇒l=2×3404×170=1 m
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
